title: Aluminum alloy products and methods for making the same
abstract: New aluminum alloys are disclosed. Broadly, the new aluminum alloys generally include from 1.2 to 4.1 at. % Fe, from 0.2 to 1.1 at. % of Class X elements, where the at. % Fe plus at. % Class X elements is from 2.3 to 4.3 at. %, and from 0.9 to 2.5 at. % Si. The Class X elements generally comprise at least one of vanadium (V), molybdenum (Mo), niobium (Nb), tantalum (Ta), and tungsten (W). The new aluminum alloys generally include an amount of the Fe, the Class X elements, and the Si falling within an area given in FIG. 1.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An aluminum alloy comprising: (a) from 1.2 to 4.1 at. % Fe; (b) from 0.2 to 1.1 at. % of Class X elements; wherein the Class X elements comprise at least one of vanadium (V), molybdenum (Mo), niobium (Nb), tantalum (Ta), and tungsten (W); wherein at. % Fe plus at. % Class X elements is from 2.3 to 4.3 at. %; and (c) from 0.9 to 2.5 at. % Si; wherein the aluminum alloy comprises an amount of the Fe, the Class X elements, and the Si falling within an area on a graph having (at. % Fe plus at. % Class X) content on one axis and having (at. % Si) on another axis, the area being defined by the following corners: 2. The aluminum alloy of claim 1, wherein the aluminum alloy comprises not greater than 2.4 at. % Si, or not greater than 2.3 at. % Si, or not greater than 2.2 at. % Si, or not greater than 2.1 at. % Si, or not greater than 2.0 at. % Si. 3. The aluminum alloy of claim 1, wherein the aluminum alloy comprises at least 1.0 at. % Si, or at least 1.1 at. % Si, or at least 1.2 at. % Si, or at least 1.3 at. % Si. 4. The aluminum alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein at. % Fe plus at. % Class X elements is not greater than 4.2 at. %, or not greater than 4.1 at. %, or not greater than 4.0 at. %, or not greater than 3.95 at. %, or not greater than 3.9 at. %, or not greater than 3.85 at. %, or not greater than 3.8 at. %, or not greater than 3.75 at. %. 5. The aluminum alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the aluminum alloy comprises from 12 mol. % to 24 mol. % of a-AlFeSi phase. 6. The aluminum alloy of claim 5, wherein the aluminum alloy comprises not greater than 22 mol. % of a-AlFeSi phase, or not greater than 20 mol. % of a-AlFeSi phase, or not greater than 18 mol. % of a-AlFeSi phase. 7. The aluminum alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the aluminum alloy comprises not greater than 0.05 mol. % of A (Fe, Mn) phases. 8. The aluminum alloy of claim 7, wherein the aluminum alloy comprises not greater than 0.04 mol. % of Ah,(Fe, Mn) phases, or not greater than 0.03 mol. % of Ah,(Fe, Mn) phases, or not greater than 0.02 mol. % phases, or not greater than 0.01 mol. % of Ah,(Fe, Mn) phases, or wherein the aluminum alloy comprises 0% Ale(Fe, Mn) phases. 9. The aluminum alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the aluminum alloy comprises up to 4.0 at. % of Class Z elements, wherein the Class Z elements comprise at least one of copper (Cu), magnesium (Mg), zinc (Zn), lithium (Li), and silver (Ag). 10. The aluminum alloy of claim 9, wherein the aluminum alloy comprises up to 3.0 at. % of Class Z elements, or up to 2 at. % of Class Z elements, or up to 1 at. % of Class Z elements. 11. The aluminum alloy of claim 1, wherein the aluminum alloy comprises not greater than 0.25 at. % of Class Z elements. 12. The aluminum alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the aluminum alloy comprises up to 3.0 at. % of Class H elements, wherein the Class H elements comprise at least one of titanium (Ti), zirconium (Zr), and hafnium (Hf). 13. The aluminum alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the aluminum alloy comprises up to 1.0 at. % of Class E metals, wherein the Class E metals comprise at least one of indium (In), tin (Sn), bismuth (Bi), and lead (Pb). 14. The aluminum alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the aluminum alloy comprises from 1.2 to 4.1 at. % of transition metal substitutes as a whole or partial replacement for iron, wherein the transition metal substitutes comprise at least one of Cr, Mn, Co, and Ni. 15. The aluminum alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the aluminum alloy comprises up to 2.0 at. % of rare earth elements, or not greater than 1.5 at. % of rare earth elements, or not greater than 1.0 at. % of rare earth elements, or not greater than 0.75 at. % of rare earth elements, or not greater than 0.5 at. % of rare earth elements, or not greater than 0.25 at. % of rare earth elements, or not greater than 0.1 at. % of rare earth elements, or not greater than 0.05 at. % of rare earth elements. 16. The aluminum alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the balance of the aluminum alloy is aluminum, optional incidental elements and impurities. 17. An aluminum alloy product made from the aluminum alloy of any of the preceding claims. 18. The aluminum alloy product of claim 17, wherein the aluminum alloy product is an additively manufactured product. 19. The aluminum alloy product of claim 17, wherein the aluminum alloy product is an additive manufacturing feedstock. 20. The aluminum alloy product of claim 17, wherein the aluminum alloy product is a wrought product. 21. The aluminum alloy product of claim 17, wherein the wrought product is one of a sheet product, a plate product, a foil product, a forged product, and an extruded product. 22. The aluminum alloy product of claim 17, wherein the aluminum alloy product is an ingot or billet. 23. The aluminum alloy product of claim 17, wherein the aluminum alloy product is a shape cast product. 24. The aluminum alloy product of claim 17, wherein the aluminum alloy product is a powder metallurgy product. 25. The aluminum alloy product of any of the preceding claims, wherein the aluminum alloy product comprises a fine eutectic-type microstructure. 26. The aluminum alloy product of claim 25, wherein the aluminum alloy product comprises at least one of spheroidal, cellular, lamellar, and wavy structures. 27. The aluminum alloy of any of claims 25-26, wherein the fine eutectic-structure realizes an average eutectic spacing of not greater than 10 micrometers, or not greater than 8 micrometers, or not greater than 6 micrometers, or not greater than 5 micrometers, or not greater than 4 micrometers, or not greater than 3 micrometers, or not greater than 2 micrometers, or not greater than 1 micrometers, or not greater than 0.5 micrometers. 28. The aluminum alloy product of any of the preceding claims, wherein the aluminum alloy product comprises equiaxed grains having an average grain size of from 0.05 to 50 microns. 29. The aluminum alloy of claim 28, wherein the aluminum alloy comprises at least 50 vol. % of the equiaxed grains, or at least 60 vol. % of the equiaxed grains, or at least 70 vol. % of the equiaxed grains, or at least 80 vol. % of the equiaxed grains, or at least 90 vol. 

 % of the equiaxed grains, or at least 95 vol. % of the equiaxed grains, or at least 99 vol. % of the equiaxed grains. 30. The aluminum alloy of any of claims 28-29, wherein the equiaxed grains realize an average grain size of not greater than 30 microns, or not greater than 20 microns, or not greater than 10 microns, or not greater than 5 microns, or not greater than 4 microns, or not greater than 3 microns, or not greater than 2 microns. 31. The aluminum alloy of any of claims 1-27, wherein the aluminum alloy comprises columnar grains. 32. The aluminum alloy of any of the preceding claims, wherein the aluminum alloy is in the form of an engine component for an aerospace or automotive vehicle. 33. The aluminum alloy of claim 32, wherein the engine component is a compressor wheel for a turbocharger. 34. The aluminum alloy of claim 32, wherein the engine component is one of a heat exchanger and a piston.
